| Property | Value | Relevance to CYP2C9 |
|---|---|---|
| MW | 119.08 Da | Very small — poor active site occupancy |
| LogP | −0.26 | Hydrophilic — CYP2C9 prefers moderate lipophilicity |
| TPSA | 80.44 Å² | High relative to size |
| HBA | 3 | Present but insufficient without aromatic scaffold |
| HBD | 1 | Low |
| Aromatic rings | **0** | Critical absence — CYP2C9 substrates/inhibitors nearly always aromatic |
| QED | 0.41 | Low drug-likeness |
The ML models returned a **CYP2C9 inhibitor probability of 0.00** — essentially zero. For context across all CYP isoforms:

| CYP Isoform | Inhibition Probability |
|---|---|
| **CYP2C9** | **0.00** |
| CYP1A2 | 0.00 |
| CYP2C19 | 0.03 |
| CYP3A4 | 0.00 |
| CYP2D6 | 0.50 (indeterminate) |
**Why this molecule will NOT inhibit CYP2C9:**

1. **No aromatic system.** CYP2C9 has a predominantly hydrophobic active site cavity that accommodates planar aromatic scaffolds. Known CYP2C9 inhibitors (warfarin, fluconazole, sulfaphenazole) all contain extended aromatic/heteroaromatic ring systems that stack within the active site. 3-Nitropropanoic acid is a fully aliphatic 4-atom chain — it simply cannot make the pi-stacking and hydrophobic contacts required.

2. **Too small and hydrophilic.** At MW 119 and LogP −0.26, this molecule is far below the typical pharmacophore space for CYP2C9 inhibitors (usually MW 200–500, LogP 1–4). It would have poor binding affinity due to insufficient van der Waals contacts with the active site walls.

3. **No recognized CYP2C9 pharmacophore.** The molecule lacks sulfonamide, coumarin, oxicam, or any extended conjugated system. While it has hydrogen bond acceptors (COOH, NO₂), these alone cannot drive CYP2C9 binding without an appropriate hydrophobic scaffold to anchor the molecule in the active site.

- The ML model prediction (probability = 0.00) is fully consistent with the structural analysis.
- No contradictions found — all lines of evidence converge.
- The only uncertainty is the nitro group, which can be metabolically active, but nitro groups alone (without an aromatic carrier) are not associated with CYP2C9 inhibition.
**PREDICTION: negative** — this molecule is NOT an inhibitor of CYP2C9.
**CONFIDENCE: high** — structural analysis and ML prediction are in complete agreement; the molecule lacks every key feature associated with CYP2C9 inhibition.

**KEY_FACTORS:**
1. **Complete absence of aromatic rings** — CYP2C9 inhibitors require planar aromatic systems for active site binding
2. **Very low molecular weight and negative LogP** — too small and hydrophilic to occupy the CYP2C9 binding pocket
3. **No CYP2C9 pharmacophore elements** — lacks sulfonamides, coumarins, oxicams, or any extended conjugated scaffold
